questions.text: Batch Management in Direct Flow can be shipped before the quality controls are finished.
All stocked materials at L'Oréal are followed by batch management.
When the stock is in block status, it will keep the precise batch in SAP.
The SLED (Shelf-Life Expiration Date) is calculated based on the date of manufacturing plus the total shelf life.
Product manufactured by L'Oréal factory (FERT Material) which are not batch-managed will not require SLED date.
Batch number and values in the batch characteristics will be the same for all plants that have received the batch.
The Movement Code for Goods Receipt from purchasing is Mvt = 601 and the movement code for Goods Issue from a sales process is Mvt = 101.
Factory GLN is the factory identification number where the batch was shipped.
Once the “Date of Manufacturing” is filled, the system will calculate the SLED date by performing a simple addition: Date of manufacturing + Total days.
The Inspection Type for Direct Flow material is Z01DIRPF, and it should not be set as preferred or ticked to trigger GR.
“Planned Release Date (QM)” corresponds to the “End date” of the quality control on the factory side and it is the real date of stock availability.
IF the SAP standard « Delivery date » is LONGER than the « planned release date » then the Date considered in stock requirement FIORI applications and APO will be the Planned Release Date
The UD Code D006 represents a rejected Usage Decision, in this case, the stock status equals “Blocked” and the batch status is “Unrestricted”.

Batch derivation needs to be maintained using 2 SAP standard transactions: 
DVR1: Maintain the criteria for the sending batch(es): BOM components
DVS1: Maintain the criteria for the receiving batch: BOM Header
With Batch Master in Direct Flow, the batch is created and/or updated in WISE (Factory SAP system) and all the updates will be automatically transferred to NEO instance via HIP
Batch management in NEO is only activated for materials that are stocked.
All stocked materials in NEO are tracked by batch.
The batch number and batch characteristics are unique for each material/batch combination.
The SLED (Shelf-Life Expiration Date) is calculated based on the date of manufacturing plus the total shelf life.
L'Oréal utilizes multiple batch classes within NEO to accommodate various product types and categories.
In the Non-Direct flow scenario, the stock can be moved to AFM after production, without awaiting any UD taken from WISE plant.
The UD status for Non-Direct flow inspection lots can be modified by the entity in AFP or AFM.
When the batch is created in WISE Batch Master, the interface will update the batch UD characteristic in AFP and update the batch master in AFM.
The UD Code ‘D006’ represents a “Rejected” usage decision. In this case, the stock status equals “Blocked” and the batch status is “Unrestricted”.
When the UD ‘D003’ is taken by WISE plant for the batch, the status in AFP will be “Unrestricted” and stock “Blocked”.
